sections.paragraph:
λειποτακτέω or λιποτακτέω (s. λείπω, τάξις; milit. t.t. ‘leave the ranks, desert’; Plut., Mor. 241a; Polemo, Decl. 2, 44 p. 31, 17; PLips 45, 18; 46, 15; PLond III, 1247, 14 p. 225 [IV a.d.]; 4 Macc 9:23) transf. sense turn away ἀπό τινος from someth. ἀπὸ τοῦ θελήματος (θεοῦ) 1 Cl 21:4 (cp. Philo, Gig. 43 [268] καλὸν δὲ μὴ λιποτακτῆσαι τῆς τοῦ θεοῦ τάξεως).—DELG s.v. τάσσω.

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

PLips PLips = Griech. Urkunden der Papyrussammlung zu Leipzig—List 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

transf. transf. = transferred

someth. someth. = something

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 590
